title: Batched Inference at Scale
concept: Serving user queries requires dynamic batching, grouping incoming text requests on the fly to maximize GPU parallelization.

exercises.prompt: Why are GPUs so good at handling batches of queries?

options.text: They process things strictly one by one.
They excel at processing many calculations at the same time.
They only work when there is a single user.

exercises.explain: GPUs are designed to perform many calculations at the exact same time (in parallel), which makes them perfect for processing batches of data.

cards.title: Serving the Crowd
cards.body: When millions of people chat with an AI, the server doesn't process questions one by one. If it did, the system would crash! Instead, it uses a clever trick called dynamic batching to handle crowds instantly.

cards.title: What is Dynamic Batching?
cards.body: Dynamic batching is a server-side technique that automatically groups incoming, individual user queries into a single batch over a tiny window of time. This allows the GPU to process them all in parallel, speeding up response times for everyone.

cards.title: The Pizza Oven Strategy
cards.body: Imagine a busy pizza restaurant. If the chef bakes every single pizza slice one by one as they are ordered, the massive oven stays mostly empty and wastes heat. Instead, the chef waits a few seconds to gather multiple orders, slides five pizzas into the oven all at once, and bakes them together. This is dynamic batching!

cards.title: Batching in Action
cards.body: Imagine three users ask an AI questions at 12:00:01. Instead of starting three separate slow tasks, the server pauses for a tiny millisecond, packages the three questions together into a single batch, and sends them to the GPU. The GPU solves all three in the time it would usually take to solve one!

cards.title: Does Batching Slow Things Down?
cards.body:
⚡ MYTH: Dynamic batching makes users wait much longer for their answers.
✔️ REALITY: The waiting window is incredibly short (often just a few milliseconds). The time saved by letting the GPU process queries in parallel far outweighs this tiny initial wait!

cards.title: Single vs. Batched Serving
cards.body: See how grouping requests compares to handling them one by one.

right.text: Queries are grouped on the fly, utilizing the GPU's full parallel power to answer everyone faster.

left.text: GPU runs below capacity, processing queries sequentially while users wait in a long queue.

exercises.prompt: What happens if an AI server does NOT use dynamic batching during high traffic?

options.text: It processes queries one by one, wasting the GPU's parallel power.
It automatically translates the queries to different languages.

exercises.explain: Without dynamic batching, the GPU would run at low capacity, processing one query at a time while wasting its massive parallel computing power.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Users must manually group their questions together before sending them to a server that uses dynamic batching.
exercises.explain: Dynamic batching occurs automatically on the server side; users send their queries normally without needing to group them.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete how dynamic batching helps the GPU.
exercises.sentence: Dynamic batching groups incoming queries together so they can run in ________ on the GPU.
exercises.explain: Dynamic batching groups multiple queries together so they can run in parallel on the GPU, maximizing the hardware's efficiency.

exercises.prompt: Identify the system using dynamic batching to serve AI queries.

options.text: System A: The server processes each user's query the exact millisecond it arrives, one by one.
System B: The server holds incoming queries for 4 milliseconds to bundle them into a group for the GPU.
System C: The server asks users to wait and submit their questions in groups of five.

exercises.explain: System B uses AI-assisted dynamic batching because it automatically groups separate incoming user questions on the fly to maximize efficiency. System A does not batch, and System C relies on manual user effort.

exercises.prompt: Your AI application has suddenly gone viral, and thousands of users are asking questions at the same time. How does dynamic batching help your server handle this massive traffic?

options.text: It forces users to send their queries in large groups instead of one at a time.
It disables parallel processing to save GPU power.
It groups individual incoming queries on the fly to maximize GPU parallel processing.
It processes every single query immediately one by one, ignoring the rest.

exercises.explain: Dynamic batching is crucial for scaling because it automatically groups individual incoming requests into a single batch, letting the GPU process them in parallel without forcing users to wait too long.
